name: Metric (SI) prefixes
description: The SI decimal prefixes, tested both ways: symbol → name & power of ten, and back. Tracked separately in each direction.
primer:
**Metric prefixes** scale a unit by powers of ten. Each prefix has a **symbol**, a **name**, and a **power of $10$**. This deck tests both directions: symbol → name & power, and name/power → symbol.

### Multiples (≥ 1)

| Symbol | Name | Power |
|---|---|---|
| E | exa | $10^{18}$ |
| P | peta | $10^{15}$ |
| T | tera | $10^{12}$ |
| G | giga | $10^{9}$ |
| M | mega | $10^{6}$ |
| k | kilo | $10^{3}$ |
| h | hecto | $10^{2}$ |
| da | deca | $10^{1}$ |

### Submultiples (< 1)

| Symbol | Name | Power |
|---|---|---|
| d | deci | $10^{-1}$ |
| c | centi | $10^{-2}$ |
| m | milli | $10^{-3}$ |
| μ | micro | $10^{-6}$ |
| n | nano | $10^{-9}$ |
| p | pico | $10^{-12}$ |
| f | femto | $10^{-15}$ |
| a | atto | $10^{-18}$ |

### Watch the case and look-alikes

- **k** (kilo) is lowercase — uppercase **K** is the kelvin.
- **m** (milli) is lowercase — the metre uses the same letter as a unit.
- **M** (mega) is uppercase.
- **da** (deca) is the only **two-letter** prefix symbol.
- **μ** (micro) is the Greek letter *mu*; often typed as 'u' on keyboards.

The big prefixes above mega step by $10^{3}$ each (k, M, G, T, P, E); the small ones below milli mirror them (m, μ, n, p, f, a).

cards.note: Lowercase k — uppercase K is the kelvin.
Lowercase m — the metre uses the same letter as a unit.
Greek mu; sometimes written 'u' on keyboards.
